locked_facts: Period T = 2π√(L/g) — depends on length and g ONLY
Period is INDEPENDENT of mass of bob
Period is INDEPENDENT of amplitude (for small angles < 15°)
Restoring force = mg·sin(θ) ≈ mgθ for small angles
Maximum KE at equilibrium, maximum PE at extreme positions
Total mechanical energy is conserved throughout the motion
truth_statements: The time period of a simple pendulum depends on the length of the string and acceleration due to gravity
The time period is independent of the mass of the bob and the amplitude of oscillation for small angles
The motion of a simple pendulum is simple harmonic for small amplitudes
At the mean position, kinetic energy is maximum and potential energy is minimum
The restoring force is proportional to sin θ, approximated as θ for small angles

aha_moment.what_to_show: Two pendulums side by side — different bob masses but same string length — swinging in perfect synchrony with identical period labels displayed above each
aha_moment.visual_emphasis: Both pendulums perfectly in sync despite visually different bob sizes — period readouts are identical numbers
aha_moment.why_this_breaks_misconception: Students see that a heavy bob and a light bob complete each swing at exactly the same time — mass does not matter

simulation_states.label: Setup
simulation_states.description: Single pendulum at rest, displaced to initial angle θ₀. Length L labelled, bob mass m labelled, gravity g arrow shown.
simulation_states.emphasis: Show the key variables: L, m, g, and θ₀ clearly labelled

simulation_states.label: Buildup
simulation_states.description: Pendulum swings. Energy bar chart shows KE and PE exchanging — KE max at bottom, PE max at extremes. Period T is measured and displayed.
simulation_states.emphasis: Energy conservation — total energy bar stays constant while KE and PE bars oscillate

simulation_states.label: Aha
simulation_states.description: Two pendulums appear — same L, different mass bobs (one heavy, one light). Both swing in perfect synchrony. Period labels above each show identical values.
simulation_states.emphasis: Both pendulums swing together — mass does NOT affect period. T = 2π√(L/g) has no mass term.

simulation_states.label: Explore
simulation_states.description: Interactive: adjust string length and bob mass independently. Period updates in real time. Changing mass has zero effect on T. Changing L changes T visibly.
simulation_states.emphasis: Drag the mass slider — period does not change. Drag the length slider — period changes. This proves T depends only on L and g.

common_misconceptions.belief: A heavier bob swings faster because gravity pulls it more
trigger_phrases: heavy bob kam time lagta hai
mass badhao toh jaldi swing karega
bhari bob jaldi jaldi hilta hai
common_misconceptions.correction: NCERT says the time period T = 2π√(L/g) depends only on length and g — mass does not appear in the formula at all
common_misconceptions.simulation_emphasis: Two pendulums with different bob masses but same length swinging in perfect synchrony — period readouts identical

legend.meaning: Kinetic and potential energy (sum is constant)
